note: Excerpt only. AOP-80-pro-no-go came from an insufficient short Pro ask and is superseded for decision-making by this fuller dual-review request.

entries.summary: Hermes control-plane inventory identified the central drift: live Multica chief metadata did not expose the true model, MCP, and skills capability surface.

artifact_refs.note: Later live snapshot confirms MCP and skills drift remains high.

entries.decision: Use inventory drift as a hard input to the manifest and transition checker work.
risk_notes: Multica-visible metadata is not yet complete enough to be the sole source of truth.

entries.summary: Hermes auth lane was proven isolated from daily Codex/Codex2 TUI lanes by token fingerprint comparison.
entries.decision: Treat auth isolation as necessary but insufficient; behavior still needs manifest, command scope, and transition policy.
risk_notes: This ledger stores only proof references and does not store auth fingerprints or token values.

entries.summary: Hermes chief prompt/routing/escalation contract separated chief, red-team, sidecar, and worker responsibilities.
entries.decision: Accept as role design baseline only; execution authority remains blocked pending manifest/checker artifacts.
blocked: implicit specialist handoff without issue contract
risk_notes: A role prompt is not an enforcement boundary; the checker must carry hard gates.

entries.summary: Chief manifest, drift detector contract, transition checker contract, no-op rule, and red-team independence policy were accepted as design baseline.

review_refs.note: Sidecar narrowed ASF-18 to design-baseline acceptance.
GAC converted conditional no-go to go for design baseline.

entries.decision: Proceed to runnable dry-run harness; do not treat ASF-18 markdown as compiled policy.
enabled: ASF-23 runnable manifest and advance_one dry-run harness
risk_notes: The original red-team concern was overclaiming; this is recorded as design, not implementation.

entries.summary: Planning-memory Harness0 case_01 produced a red-team-review-ready artifact bundle in the planning repo; no maint checkpoint file was found.

artifact_refs.note: Completion comment names the artifact directory and file list.
Directory listing verified with README.md, input pack, run manifest, rubric result, memory delta candidate, and resume proof.
AOP-70 blocker resolved by recording the planning artifact bundle rather than inventing a checkpoint.

entries.decision: Use the planning artifact bundle as the proof artifact and keep the missing maint checkpoint as an explicit known gap.
enabled: proof ledger can include ASF-22 with gap rationale
blocked: claiming ASF-22 has a maint checkpoint
using ASF-22 as production memory proof
risk_notes: This proof lives outside maint; future bridge work should import a stable summary or evidence pointer.

entries.summary: Runnable chief manifest and advance_one dry-run harness implemented fixture-only no-op/propose-transition policy checks with no live mutation.
entries.decision: Accept dry-run harness as compiled policy proof, but only for static snapshots and fixtures.
risk_notes: The harness does not acquire live state by itself; ASF-24 adds read-only input acquisition.

entries.summary: Read-only live snapshot exporter implemented sanitized Multica/Hermes input acquisition for the dry-run harness.
entries.decision: Accept read-only snapshot exporter. Continue to block transitions while MCP/skills drift remains high.
enabled: proof ledger and sidecar knowledge extraction
risk_notes: Live issue fields use conservative defaults; every live candidate fails closed until richer contracts are encoded.

entries.summary: GAC red-team review challenged ASF-18 overclaiming and forced design-spec framing, input acquisition caveat, and deterministic candidate ordering.
entries.decision: Accept GAC as independent gate for ASF-18 design baseline.
blocked: claiming markdown contract is compiled policy
risk_notes: Initial no-go was useful; gate quality depends on not downgrading high-risk reviews to Codex fallback.

entries.summary: GAC implementation review accepted ASF-23 dry-run harness with no blocking concerns.
entries.decision: Accept ASF-23 implementation as fixture-only dry-run proof.
risk_notes: Review noted low-severity schema tightening opportunities for future revisions.

entries.summary: GAC implementation review accepted ASF-24 snapshot exporter with no blocking concerns.
entries.decision: Accept ASF-24 and proceed to proof ledger / sidecar knowledge extraction.
risk_notes: Non-blocking concerns include API proxy endpoint visibility, UUID validation, and value scanning if allowlist expands.

entries.summary: Pro extended advisory ruled No-Go for live Hermes chief MCP/skills reconciliation while independent GAC/Claude gate is unavailable.

review_refs.note: Pro extended answer completed with No-Go for live reconciliation and Go only for static/read-only work.

entries.decision: Do not write agent.mcp_config, create or assign chief workspace skills, relax source-of-truth checks, or mark high drift resolved until an independent gate or explicit user authorization exists.
blocked: using Codex Grade-C fallback as high-risk approval
risk_notes: MCP/skills drift touches the chief capability surface and source-of-truth contract.
Restrictive skill assignment is still live prompt and behavior mutation.
Writing non-executable summaries into mcp_config would pollute an executable-semantics field.

known_gaps.description: ASF-22 has no maint checkpoint file matching docs/checkpoints/*asf22*, but it has an external planning artifact bundle and a Multica completion comment.
known_gaps.current_handling: Record the planning artifact directory and completion comment as proof refs; do not claim a maint checkpoint exists.

known_gaps.description: GAC reviews in assistant-ops sometimes cannot directly read assistant-factory issue bodies unless target context is copied into the review issue.
known_gaps.current_handling: Copy required contracts and artifact paths into every red-team review issue until a cross-workspace evidence bridge exists.

known_gaps.description: The dry-run harness and live snapshot exporter are intentionally not wired to execute transitions.
known_gaps.current_handling: Treat no-op as success; require a separate reviewed implementation before any live mutation path is added.

known_gaps.description: GAC/Claude red-team currently fails with quota/402 for high-risk chief metadata review, and Codex GPT-5.5 xhigh fallback is only Grade-C.
known_gaps.current_handling: Keep Hermes chief MCP/skills reconciliation blocked; allow only static proposal, evidence, proof-ledger, and gate-packet work until independent gate or explicit user authorization exists.
